title: Vanguards of Health Care: Cortica’s Whole-Child Autism Bet

summary: Daniel Alegre, CEO, TelevisaUnivision discusses global sports media economics and Spanish-language audience growth with Bloomberg’s Dani Burger at Bloomberg House Miami 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Yen Soars as Traders Say Japan Intervened After ‘Final’ Warning
summary: The yen surged as much as 3%, its biggest intraday gain in almost two years, as traders and local media said that Japan intervened in the foreign-exchange market, hours after officials delivered a “final” warning to investors against selling the currency.

title: Treasuries Draw Buyers After Selloff as Oil Surge Stalls
summary: Treasuries rose as the recent surge in oil prices stalled and bond investors were drawn to 30-year yields that topped 5% for the first time this year.

title: ECB Officials See June Hike If Energy Costs Don’t Ease First
summary: European Central Bank policymakers are likely to raise interest rates at their next meeting in June unless there are positive developments on energy prices and ending the Iran war, according to people familiar with the situation.

title: The David Rubenstein Show: Charlie Scharf
summary: Wells Fargo Chairman & CEO Charlie Scharf says the independence of the Federal Reserve is "critically important" to the US financial system, even as presidents weigh in on policy. In this wide-ranging interview, Scharf discusses the state of US economy amid tensions with Iran, as well as the risks and opportunities in private credit and AI. Scharf is on this week's episode of "The David Rubenstein Show: Peer to Peer Conversations."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Meta Gets $96 Billion of Orders for Its Latest Jumbo Bond Sale
summary: Investors submitted about $96 billion of orders for Meta Platforms Inc.’s bond sale, according to people with knowledge of the transaction, as the Facebook parent boosts spending on infrastructure for the artificial intelligence boom.

title: Banamex Selling Global Bonds for First Time Since Citi Sale
summary: Mexico’s Grupo Financiero Banamex is tapping global markets for the first time since Citigroup Inc. started offloading its stake in the bank.

title: Audi F1 CEO Tries to Turn Also-Ran Legacy Team Into a Winner
summary: Audi’s Formula 1 boss said entering the sport via a legacy team has proved more difficult than starting with a clean slate, as the German carmaker runs up against more established rivals.

title: Hospitals Sell Muni Debt at Fastest Pace in More Than a Decade
summary: Hospitals across the country are bracing for financial pain ahead of sweeping Medicaid cuts from the Trump administration. But that hasn’t stopped them from borrowing at the fastest pace in more than a decade.

title: Pemex Posts Third Straight Loss Despite War-Fueled Oil Rally
summary: Petroleos Mexicanos posted its third straight quarterly loss as surging global oil prices failed to boost the debt-laden oil giant’s finances amid slumping production and refineries that are bleeding cash.

title: US Stocks Rise on Earnings, on Course for Best Month Since 2020
summary: US stocks advanced on Thursday as investors focused on resilient US economic growth and corporate earnings while oil prices fell from a four-year high.

title: Junk Firms Are Locking In Fixed-Rate Deals Before Costs Rise
summary: High-risk borrowers in Europe are taking advantage of cheaper fixed-rate bonds to refinance floating-rate debt, cutting costs and buying protection against the risk of interest rate hikes.

title: Copper Holds Steady as Traders Weigh Risk of Protracted Conflict
summary: Copper held steady as signs of rising demand in China were offset by growing worries about the possibility of an escalation in the US-Israeli war against Iran.

title: New York Life Eyes Potential Canadian Dollar Debt Sale
summary: New York Life Global Funding is weighing a Canadian-dollar debt offering as soon as Thursday, according to people familiar with the matter, who asked not to be identified as they aren’t authorized to speak publicly.

title: Taiwan's Cheng Li-wun Seeks Trump Meeting After Beijing Trip
summary: Cheng Li-wun discusses her hope to meet President Trump following her Beijing visit, aiming to convey Taiwan's commitment to avoiding conflict and securing lasting cross-strait peace. (Includes partial English translation from :35.) (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Audi on Building Its Formula 1 Team
summary: Mattia Binotto, CEO & Team Principal, Audi Revolut F1 Team discusses building a fully integrated Formula 1 team and evolving Sauber with Bloomberg’s Matt Miller at Bloomberg House Miami 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Jana Pushes Markel to Divest Ventures, Buy Back $2 Billion Shares
summary: Jana Partners is renewing its push for Markel Group Inc. to divest its venture arm while pushing the insurer to also buy back $2 billion of its shares to boost the stock.

title: Bank of England Holds Rates as Officials Consider Hikes Ahead
summary: The Bank of England kept interest rates on hold with several policymakers saying they might consider future hikes, just as oil prices soared within reach of the central bank’s most pessimistic scenario for the economy.

title: European Power Plunges to Record Negative Prices on Solar Surge
summary: European power prices slid to record lows for Friday, with hugely negative hourly rates in Germany and France, as strong solar generation coincides with public holidays across much of the region.

title: Stocks Edge Higher as Oil Prices Ease; JPMorgan 's Meera Pandit
summary: Mira Pandit of JPMorgan Asset Management discussed the current earnings outlook amid easing oil prices and ongoing geopolitical tensions. Despite oil pulling back from wartime highs, JPMorgan analysts project that oil prices between $110 and $120 per barrel over six months could reduce earnings per share by 4 to 6%, yet still support double-digit earnings growth overall. Earnings growth expectations have been revised upward from 15% to nearly 19% year-over-year, driven primarily by energy and resilient technology sectors. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Retraces From Four-Year High as War Fears Dampen Liquidity
summary: Brent crude cooled off after ripping to a four-year high on anticipation of a protracted US war with Iran, in a session marked by machine-led trading and thin volumes.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 4/30/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Rebounds as Speculation of Yen Intervention Weakens Dollar
summary: Gold bounced back from a three-day decline, as the dollar sank and traders weighed the prospect of a fresh US military engagement in Iran.
